data.body:
Plan-review re-run at HEAD b76dd09a1 → PASS (0 blocking, 15 advisory). Triage of advisories before implementation:

- [MAJOR, honored in WS2] 'cached boolean' vs 'name permanently-failing units' inconsistency: the per-process pending cache will hold the SET of pending ids (registry_ids − parsed .ensure-applied), not a bool, so the rate-limited hint can name specific pending/failing units. ≤1 read/proc preserved (set computed once, cached).
- [minor, WS3] MCP-startup lock-held behavior: run_ensures at boot uses a short write-lock timeout and log-and-continues on LockTimeout/any error — never blocks or aborts boot.
- [minor, noted] fresh-init lock nesting is safe: _acquire_init_lock is the ticket-init.lock MKDIR lock; run_ensures takes the distinct .ticket-write.lock store write lock — no self-deadlock.
- [minor, WS1] units appear on BOTH re-init (209-213) and fresh-init (242-248): run_ensures replaces the hand-listed calls on both paths + symlink-init env-id callers; a registry-id frozen-set test guards against double-execution/rename.
- [minor, WS1/WS5] zero-commit idempotency test overlap: authoritative git-log idempotency test lives in WS5; WS1's test asserts registry shape + init wiring.
- [minor→none, WS4] docs/migrations.md will additionally cover the [ensure] config section (hint_interval_secs/hint_enabled) and the rationale for excluding inline-committer paths (compact/txn/transition_close/delete).
- [none, WS1/WS4] post-rollback: committed .gitattributes/.gitignore content stays on the tickets branch (harmless/idempotent); git-ignored .ensure-applied/.ensure-hinted are ignored by old code.
- [minor, WS1] init callers do not currently rely on ensure-step exceptions; run_ensures skip-and-continues and init logs failures, never aborts.

ac-quality (compound 'and' criteria) acknowledged as cosmetic; ACs left as-is (still independently verifiable in practice).
